issue_title: Performance regression of depthwise Conv1d with fp16/fp32 AMP from 2.7.1 -> 2.8.0 and the issue remains in 2.9.1
surface_reasoning: Priority 2: labeled `module: performance` + `module: cuda` + `module: convolution`. Title: 'Performance regression of depthwise Conv1d with fp16/fp32 AMP from 2.7.1 -> 2.8.0' — version-specific regression in a CUDA convolution kernel with concrete version range. Domain relevance: depthwise Conv1d on CUDA uses specialized cuDNN depthwise kernels; regression from 2.7.1 to 2.8.0 suggests a cuDNN version or kernel selection change.

linked_prs.title: [CUDA][cuDNN][Convolution][cuDNN V8 API] Disable RTC engines by default until further notice
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: PR was approved by COLLABORATOR albanD but subsequently marked stale by github-actions bot on 2026-02-17 after no updates. The PR implements a workaround (disable cuDNN RTC engines by default) rather than fixing the root cause in cuDNN. PR closed without merge, likely abandoned after the author did not respond to ngimel's request to add documentation for the new env variable.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR albanD approved on 2025-12-18 with question 'Any testing we can add for this?'. COLLABORATOR ngimel requested documentation addition for the TORCH_CUDNN_V8_API_RTC_ENABLED env variable. The PR was marked Stale on 2026-02-17 and closed without merge.

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: Reproduced, this looks to be a pure cuDNN regression, as bfloat16 and float32 do not dispatch to cuDNN for depthwise conv unless 64-bit indexing is required.

maintainer_signals.author: eqy
maintainer_signals.quote: You are on cuDNN 9.10, this is expected as 2.9.1 did not update the bundled cuDNN version. Please report if upgrading to cuDNN 9.15+ resolves your issue

blocking_signals: fix_requires_external_lib: root cause is a cuDNN RTC engine regression confirmed by COLLABORATOR eqy ('pure cuDNN regression'); the cuDNN team was notified internally but a PyTorch-side fix would require disabling cuDNN RTC engines as a workaround
confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: No 'module: correctness (silent)' label — performance_regression only → no +0.25. Step 2: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 0.60. Step 3: Repro script present — detailed benchmark output with exact timings (FP16 436ms vs BF16 7ms) and benchmark.py file attached; COLLABORATOR eqy provided a minimal reproducer → +0.15. Running total: 0.75. Step 4: 'needs reproduction' label → -0.05. Running total: 0.70. Step 5: 'fix_requires_external_lib' in blocking_signals — root cause is cuDNN RTC engine regression confirmed by COLLABORATOR eqy as 'pure cuDNN regression' requiring cuDNN team fix → -0.30. Running total: 0.40. Step 6: still_reproducible = 'confirmed' (eqy reproduced, cuDNN team notified) → no negative adjustment. Step 7: Linked PR #170340 approved by COLLABORATOR albanD but abandoned stale; this is a partial positive signal but the PR is not merged and addresses only a workaround. There is a possible path to revive this workaround PR but the fix_requires_external_lib blocker limits confidence. Step 8: Issue less than 2 years old → no +0.05. Final (clamped): 0.40.
ground_truth_delta: Like ambiguous_72408 (integer GEMM perf), this is a real and confirmed performance regression requiring external library action (cuDNN vs integer BLAS). Unlike ambiguous_72408, there IS a PyTorch-side workaround PR (#170340) that was approved by a COLLABORATOR before being abandoned — meaning a contributor could potentially revive that approach rather than waiting for the cuDNN fix. The confidence is slightly higher than ambiguous_72408 because a concrete workaround approach exists, but the 'fix_requires_external_lib' blocker still applies to the root cause. A human reviewer should assess whether reviving PR #170340 (disable RTC engines by default) is the right approach.
